name: Ria Money Transfer
description_short: At 2900 E Anaheim St in Long Beach, CA 90804, you'll find Ria Money Transfer. Call +1 855-355-2141 for information.
description_long:
Ria Money Transfer is an established international remittance service with over 35 years of operating history. The company specializes in facilitating cross-border money transfers to family, friends, and recipients across more than 190 countries and territories globally. With a network exceeding 600,000 payout locations, Ria operates as a traditional money transfer service rather than a credit or lending product.

Ria's service offerings include multiple send channels: online via website, mobile app (available on iOS and Android), in-person at retail agent locations, and WhatsApp integration for direct transfers. Recipients can receive funds through bank deposit, cash pickup at agent locations, digital wallet transfers, home delivery, or ATM withdrawal. The platform supports over 100 currencies and displays real-time exchange rates. Fees and rates vary by corridor; promotional rates are offered on first transfers. The company claims transfers complete in seconds and provides real-time tracking functionality.

Ria differentiates itself through its expansive agent network (600,000+ locations), multi-channel sending capability including WhatsApp integration, and competitive positioning on exchange rates and fees. The company emphasizes its legacy (35+ years), safety record (claims over 1 billion transfers completed), and a 100% satisfaction guarantee. Ria holds official sponsorship status with the Mexican National Team, indicating significant market presence in Mexico-corridor transfers. Mobile app ratings are strong (4.9 stars on iOS, 4.5 on Trustpilot).

Honestly assessed, Ria is a solid conventional money transfer service best suited for recurring international remittances rather than emergency cash access. The service depends on agent network availability and payout method selection; while online and app options exist, actual fund access requires visiting a location or using linked banking. This positions it as a check-cashing/money transfer utility rather than a financial access solution for domestic or emergency purposes. Pricing appears competitive but varies significantly by country corridor and payment method.
address: 2900 E Anaheim St, Long Beach, CA 90804

company_info.headquarters: 2900 E Anaheim St, Long Beach, CA 90804

pros: Extensive global network with 600,000+ payout locations across 190+ countries
Multiple send channels including WhatsApp integration for direct chat-based transfers
Support for 100+ currencies with real-time exchange rate quotes displayed
Fast transfers claiming completion in seconds with real-time tracking capability
Strong mobile app ratings (4.9 stars iOS, 4.5 Trustpilot) with intuitive interface
35+ year operating history with claim of over 1 billion completed transfers
Multiple payout options: bank deposit, cash pickup, digital wallet, home delivery, ATM
cons: Exchange rates and fees vary significantly by country corridor, making comparison difficult
Requires physical location visit for cash pickup unless using bank deposit or digital wallet
No domestic money transfer or bill payment services—exclusively international transfers
Account funding limited to debit card in example shown; credit card or bank account options not clearly stated
Customer service contact information not visible on website homepage; help center required
best_for: Immigrants and expatriates sending regular remittances to family in Latin America, Mexico, or Africa
Individuals without traditional bank accounts seeking international transfer options
Users preferring WhatsApp-based transfers for convenience and direct chat integration
People in countries with large Ria agent networks seeking cash pickup payout convenience
diagnosis: Ria Money Transfer is best for immigrants and individuals regularly sending remittances internationally, particularly to Mexico and Latin America where their agent network is densest. The main caveat is that this is exclusively an international money transfer service—not a domestic solution—and actual fund access still requires either visiting a physical location, maintaining a bank account for deposits, or using digital wallets, limiting utility for pure cash-access needs.
services: Mobile app-based money transfers (iOS and Android)
In-person money transfers at retail agent locations
WhatsApp-based money transfers to select countries
